title.en: This is driving me crazy! I'm losing my mind! 미치겠네! 정말 돌아버리겠어!

description.en: When things don't go your way! Learn real reactions to express that you're "going crazy" with frustration.
description.ko: 일이 마음대로 안 풀릴 때! 너무 답답해서 "미칠 것 같은" 심정을 표현하는 리얼한 리액션을 배워봅니다.

content.speaker: John
content.text: What's up world. Welcome back to Talkori. I'm your host. John.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 안녕하세요 여러분~! 오늘도 여러분의 한국어 갈증을 풀어드릴 미나입니다.
content.translation: Hello everyone~! I'm Mina. here to quench your thirst for Korean again today.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina. There is a fly. In this studio. It keeps buzzing around my ear. Buzz..., buzz...,

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 어쩐지 아까부터 존이 계속 허공에 손을 휘두르더라구요.
content.translation: No wonder John has been waving his hands in the air for a while.

content.speaker: John
content.text: I've been trying to catch it for ten minutes! "This is driving me crazy!"

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 와~ 정말 "미치겠네요!"
content.translation: Wow~ it's driving me crazy!

insight.title: 미치겠네
insight.usage_tip: Used when you're extremely frustrated. stressed. or even when something is too good.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 저 조그만 파리 때문에 "정말 돌아버리겠어요!" 그쵸?
content.translation: You're 'losing your mind' because of that tiny fly. right?

insight.title: 돌아버리겠어
insight.usage_tip: A stronger version of 'going crazy'. meaning you're losing your mind.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Exactly! I'm about to lose it!

content.speaker: John
content.text: "Michi-get-ne". I hear this in every K-drama when the main character is stressed.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요! 한국 사람들이 제일 많이 쓰는 감탄사 중 하나예요.
content.translation: Right! It's one of the most common exclamations Koreans use.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "미치다"는 'Go crazy'라는 뜻인데..., 뒤에..., "겠네" 를 붙여서 지금 내 상태를 말하는 거예요.
content.translation: 'Michida' means 'Go crazy'. and adding 'getne' expresses your current state.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "미치겠네~!"
content.translation: I'm going crazy~!

content.speaker: John
content.text: And "돌아버리겠어". It sounds more intense.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네! 머리가 뱅글뱅글 돌 정도로 답답하다는 뜻이에요.
content.translation: Yes! It means you're so frustrated that your head is spinning.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "진짜 돌아버리겠네!" 라고 하면 정말 한계치에 다다랐을 때 쓰는 표현이죠.
content.translation: If you say 'Jinjja dora-beori-get-ne'. it's an expression for when you've reached your limit.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "정말..., 돌아버리겠어!"
content.translation: I'm really losing my mind!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Imagine I'm trying to assemble a bookshelf. but the instructions are in a language I don't know. and I have five screws left over.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존 씨. 그거 나사가 왜 다섯 개나 남았어요?
content.translation: John. why are there five screws left over?

content.speaker: John
content.text: I don't know! I followed the pictures!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "아이참~!" 다시 다 분해해야겠는데요?
content.translation: Oh boy~! You have to take it all apart again.

content.speaker: John
content.text: NO! "This is driving me crazy!"

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 와~ 진짜 "미치겠네" 정말!
content.translation: Wow~ it's really driving me crazy. honestly!

content.speaker: John
content.text: I've been doing this for three hours!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존 씨. "정말 돌아버리겠네요" 그쵸?
content.translation: John. you're really losing your mind. right?

content.speaker: John
content.text: Yes! I think I'm just going to use glue instead.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그러면 책장이 무너질 텐데요?
content.translation: Then the bookshelf will collapse. you know?

content.speaker: John
content.text: These expressions are so therapeutic to shout out loud.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 너무 스트레스받을 땐 참지 말고..., "미치겠네!"..., 라고 외쳐보세요!
content.translation: Right. When you're too stressed. don't hold it in and shout 'Michi-get-ne!'

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review the five "crazy" reactions.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number one. "미치겠네~!"
content.translation: I'm going crazy~!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number two. "진짜 미치겠어!"
content.translation: I'm really going crazy!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number three. "돌아버리겠네!"
content.translation: I'm losing my mind!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number four. "정말 돌아버리겠어!"
content.translation: I'm really losing my mind!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number five. "미치고 팔짝 뛰겠네!"
content.translation: I'm so frustrated I could jump!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분은 오늘 미칠 정도로 답답한 일 없이 행복하시길 바랄게요!
content.translation: I hope you all have a happy day without anything frustrating enough to drive you crazy!

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you next time.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 너무 좋아서 미치겠어요. (I'm going crazy because I like it so much.)

vocabulary.meaning: To turn. To spin. To go crazy
vocabulary.example: 머리가 빙글빙글 돌아요. (My head is spinning.)

vocabulary.meaning: To be frustrated. To be stuffy
vocabulary.example: 숨이 막히고 답답해요. (I feel suffocated and frustrated.)
기계를 분해해서 고쳤어요. (I took the machine apart and fixed it.)
나사가 하나 빠졌네요. (A screw is missing.)
이제 정말 제 한계예요. (This is really my limit now.)
